msgs.text:
Turn 79 — final audit: freeze the theorem, separate proof from reproducibility artifacts I re-read only the three files attached to this turn. The original target is the over-G isomorphism question for the two quotients ΓS,ΓH, where F=P⋊G, P is the free G-operator pro-2 group on x0,x1, and the two relators rS,rH define the quotients with canonical maps to G.The governing prompt asks exactly whether there is a continuous isomorphism Φ:ΓS→ΓH with πH∘Φ=πS.The right Turn 79 move is not to open another Magnus degree, another A2 -subcase, or another coefficient ghost. Turn 78 already says the proof is frozen as a certificate theorem: with ρS=rS−1, the row operators U0 and N, the residual restricted-Lie induction, the normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction, nonlinear Certificates D.1–D.4, and finite filtered Nielsen compactness, one obtains a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH, hence ΓS≅GΓH.

The new Turn 79 audit is this:The remaining “gap” is not mathematical inside the attached sweep. It is the boundary between the proof and external reproducibility artifacts.The attached history records the verifier outputs and the certificate contracts, but the current three attachments do not include the actual D.1/D.2 verifier scripts or full pivot tables. So the final manuscript should be explicit about what is proved in the sweep and what a standalone appendix must attach. This is a presentation firewall, not a new obstruction.

1. The theorem statement is now fixed

The theorem should be stated at the F-level:∃Φ:F⟶∼

Fover GwithΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup, this implies Φ(⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F)=⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F.

Therefore Φ descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,i.e.ΓS≅GΓH.This descent through the closed normal relator subgroups and the over-G compatibility are exactly the compactness conclusion recorded in the latest proof ledger.

2. The proof should stay in certificate-contract form The proof should be organized exactly as the frozen certificate dependency graph:Certificate 0 — orientation.UseρS:=rS−1.

All finite and profinite substitutions target ρS↦rH. This removes sign ambiguity.

Certificate A — right-row H1 Nielsen matrix.With right Λ=Z2 [[G]]-rows,wS=(σ+3E,−2),wH=(Eg0+3E−2,σ−E),and a global U∈GL2 (Λ) satisfies wSU=wH. On the dangerous Ef-piece, use the row operatorU0 (a,b)=(a(s−1)+b(s−2),a+2b),soU0 (s+3,−2)=(s2+1,s−1),with determinant s, a unit. This row-operator convention avoids the transpose ambiguity repeatedly flagged in the final appendix audit.Certificate B — primitive-boundary verifier.Use the reduced-coproduct construction forLn=Primn(F2⟨A,B⟩),and the boundary maps∂d:Ld⊕Ld→Ld+1,(u,v)↦[A,u]+[B,v].The attached ledger records the primitive dimensions and the boundary/secondary ranks used by the proof, including the tables through d=10.Certificate C — residual restricted-Lie induction.On the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 piece, the equation isS(Y0,Y1,q)=X2,whereS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)−1Y1−2Y03Y0q.

The obstruction quotient satisfiesLn/im∂n−1≅{0,Ln/2[2],n odd,n even,and the sweep proves Cm=Lm for m≥5. Thus the residual tower closes uniformly: odd layers are ordinary boundaries, even layers are killed by square-root transgressions.Certificate D — nonlinear finite blocks.Keep the four finite blocks, but present them as contracts rather than reopening them:

BlockD.1D.2D.3D.4

Content ker∂8↠L5[2]residual degree-16 λ-toggleA2=R/(4,t),d=4,μ4 -toggled=8 quotient witness

Certificate form rank∂8=56, rank(∂8,B10)=62η4=Y4 (X,(X,Y4))(Y,(Y,(X,Y4)))rankD5,6,7=60, rank(D5,6,7,μ4B4)=61θ80(0,B8)=(B8)[2], λ((B8)[2])=1 The latest history explicitly records D.3’s final executable stack-rank certificate 60→61, which was the last verifier gap closed inside the sweep.It also records the D.4 quotient mechanism: (0,B8) kills the residual codimension-one miss after quotienting by imθ80.Certificate E — normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction.On the Ef-coefficient ring

R=EfΛ≅Z2 [[t]],t=s−1,normalize the local row exactly:(s2+1,s−1)∼(2,t),using

N(a,b)=(a−(s+1)b,b),because(s2+1)−(s+1)(s−1)=2.After this normalization, the coefficient row is the regular Koszul row (2,t). The first t-layer is contractible; the first 2-Bockstein secondary maps satisfy Bd↠Hd(2)for d≥4; and higher (2,t)-adic layers are killed by the Magnus-degree-first Koszul–Witt cascade.Certificate F — finite filtered Nielsen compactness.For degree cutoff N and open ideal J⊂Λ, definePN,J=P∩(1+IN+JM).

Then P/PN,J is finite and ⋂N,JPN,J=1. The finite solution sets SN,J consist of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data(y0,y1,cσ,cτ)∈(P/PN,J)4 with first Magnus layer UmodJ, satisfying the finite cocycle relation for τσ=τ2, and sending rS−1 to rH modulo PN,J. This avoids both the false degree-only finite quotient and any Hopficity shortcut for the infinite pro-2 kernel. Compactness of the finite nonempty system gives a compatible inverse-limit datum, hence the desired over-G automorphism.

3. What should not happen next The sweep should not continue by testing a new degree, a new coefficient quotient, or a new exceptional finite case. That would be the wrong structural move.The repeated lesson of the previous turns is that fixed-gauge tails are often false obstructions. The correct invariant object is the full filtered Nielsen/cocycle torsor, then the certificate contract proving the torsor has the required image. Turn 78’s final skeleton already encodes this.

4. Remaining external appendix boundary The only remaining work for a standalone paper is external reproducibility:attach D.1 pivot data or verifier script for rank(∂8,B10)=62;attach D.2 pivot data or verifier script for the degree-16 λ-toggle;include the already-recorded D.3 stack-rank 60→61;include D.4’s short symbolic witness (0,B8).Inside the attached sweep, these are recorded as established. Outside this transcript, a reader would reasonably want the scripts, matrices, or pivot tables.

ESTABLISHED: Turn 79 freezes the final proof boundary. The theorem is ΓS≅GΓH, proved via an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH. The proof should be presented as Certificates 0–F, with right-row conventions and finite filtered Nielsen compactness.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains inside the attached sweep. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility/presentation: a standalone manuscript should attach the D.1/D.2 verifier artifacts or pivot tables, plus the recorded D.3 stack-rank output.

NEXT: Stop the obstruction sweep. Produce the final manuscript from the frozen certificate theorem: theorem statement, right-row convention, Certificates 0–F, verifier-output appendix, and compactness descent.[TURN79-FINAL-PROOF-FROZEN-CERTIFICATE-BOUNDARY]
